auth_note: All endpoints (except /auth/login) require JWT Bearer token. Login each user to get a token before running tests. RBAC hierarchy: VIEWER < STAFF < MANAGER < ADMIN < SUPER_ADMIN.

uuid_rules.note: Zod schema uses z.uuid() which requires valid UUID v4 format. Non-v4 UUIDs like 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000999 will fail Zod validation (400) before reaching the service layer.

discovered_entities.note: Entity IDs must be discovered at runtime via API queries. Use GET /email/messages, GET /email/templates, etc. after creating test data. Seeded email templates (INVOICE_SEND, STATEMENT_SEND, etc.) provide template IDs.

setup_dependencies: PostgreSQL with Prisma migrations applied (including 20260304080956_add_email_integration and 20260304094345_add_email_templates)
Redis running for BullMQ email-send and batch-statement-email queues
MinIO running on localhost:9000 with bucket 'nexa-attachments' (for PDF attachments)
Seeded users: admin (SUPER_ADMIN), manager (MANAGER), staff (STAFF), viewer (VIEWER)
Seeded email templates: 7 default templates (INVOICE_SEND, STATEMENT_SEND, etc.)
Environment variables: SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS (for email send tests)

test_cases.name: Create email with source entity reference - happy path
Create email with priority 1 (high) - happy path
Create email - empty recipients array (validation error)
Create email - no TO recipient (only CC) (validation error)
Create email - subject exceeds 500 chars (validation error)

body.subject: A very long subject that exceeds the maximum allowed length of five hundred characters. This subject line is intentionally made extremely long to test the Zod validation schema's max(500) constraint on the subject field. We need to ensure that the backend properly rejects requests with excessively long subjects and returns a meaningful validation error message to the client. Adding more text to ensure we definitely exceed the five hundred character limit for this particular validation test case scenario.

test_cases.name: Create email - invalid priority value (validation error)

test_cases.name: Send document email - happy path (CustomerInvoice)
test_cases.note: Requires a real CustomerInvoice in POSTED/APPROVED status. Discover ID via GET /ar/invoices first.

db_verification.query: SELECT em.status, em.source_entity_type, em.source_entity_id, em.has_attachments FROM email_messages em WHERE em.id = '{{created_email_message_id}}'
db_verification.expected: status = QUEUED, source_entity_type = CustomerInvoice, has_attachments = true

test_execution_order: 1. AUTH-01 through AUTH-04: Login all users and store tokens
2. TPL-CREATE-01, TPL-CREATE-02: Create test templates (need IDs for later tests)
3. TPL-CREATE-03 through TPL-CREATE-11: Template create validation & RBAC tests
4. TPL-LIST-01 through TPL-LIST-04: List template tests
5. TPL-GET-01 through TPL-GET-04: Get template tests
6. TPL-UPDATE-01 through TPL-UPDATE-05: Update template tests
7. TPL-PREVIEW-01 through TPL-PREVIEW-04: Preview template tests
8. EM-CREATE-01 through EM-CREATE-04: Create email messages (need IDs for later tests)
9. EM-CREATE-05 through EM-CREATE-11: Email create validation & RBAC tests
10. EM-LIST-01 through EM-LIST-07: List email tests
11. EM-GET-01 through EM-GET-04: Get email tests
12. EM-SEND-01 through EM-SEND-05: Send/queue email tests
13. EM-READ-01 through EM-READ-04: Mark read tests
14. DOCEMAIL-PREVIEW-01 through DOCEMAIL-PREVIEW-06: Document email preview tests
15. DOCEMAIL-SEND-01 through DOCEMAIL-SEND-11: Document email send tests
16. BATCH-STMT-01 through BATCH-STMT-08: Batch statement email tests
17. EM-DEL-01 through EM-DEL-04: Delete email tests (last — destroys test data)
18. TPL-DEL-01 through TPL-DEL-04: Delete template tests (last — destroys test data)

E10-1 (SMTP Outbound Service).breakdown: POST /email/messages (11), GET /email/messages (7), GET /email/messages/:id (4), POST /email/messages/:id/send (5), PATCH /email/messages/:id/read (4), DELETE /email/messages/:id (4)

E10-2 (Email Template Management).breakdown: POST /email/templates (11), GET /email/templates (4), GET /email/templates/:id (4), PATCH /email/templates/:id (5), POST /email/templates/:id/preview (4), DELETE /email/templates/:id (4)
